bugs.title: Creating a new tab hides the entire request editor (display: none)
bugs.description: When creating a new tab via Cmd+N or clicking the New Tab button, the tab-wrapping divs in App.tsx used the Tailwind `hidden` CSS class to toggle visibility. Fixed by using inline `style={{ display: tab.id === activeTabId ? 'flex' : 'none' }}`.
bugs.fixDescription: Changed from CSS class toggle to inline style attribute for tab visibility.
bugs.testStep:
1. Load the app at http://localhost:1420
2. Press Cmd+N OR click the New Tab button
3. Verify: The request editor tabs remain visible

bugs.title: Sidebar collapse width transition may leave gap or misalign layout
bugs.description: The sidebar collapse animation relies on CSS transitions which may not properly recalculate flex children when combined with tab creation. Fixed as downstream effect of bug #1.
bugs.testStep:
1. Load app at http://localhost:1420
2. Press Cmd+N
3. Click collapse sidebar button — verify layout remains aligned

bugs.title: History has duplicate entries when same request sent multiple times
bugs.description: The `addToHistory` function in `historyStore.ts` does not deduplicate entries. When the same method+URL is sent twice, two separate history entries are created with identical data (same URL, same timestamp). Additionally, the third request to a different URL (`/posts/2`) was captured in history with the wrong URL (`/posts/1`). The `addToHistory` function should check for existing entries with the same method+URL before adding, or update the existing entry instead.
bugs.fixDescription: Added dedup logic to addToHistory(): before inserting a new entry, it checks if an entry with the same method+normalized URL already exists. If found, it updates the existing entry in-place (status, responseTime, timestamp) instead of creating a duplicate. Added updateHistory() Tauri command and Rust db function for in-place updates.
bugs.testStep:
1. Load app at http://localhost:1420
2. Send GET request to `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1`
3. Send the SAME request again
4. Switch to History tab in sidebar
5. Observe: Only ONE entry for GET /posts/1 (with updated timestamp)
6. Change URL to `/posts/2` and send again
7. Observe: Two entries - one for /posts/1, one for /posts/2
